FIRM-010_CVC.notes: IPO in April 2024 limits historical data. Pre-IPO financials not publicly available. MFE used as FRE equivalent.

FIRM-011_MAN.notes: Hedge fund business model differs from PE/alt manager convention. No FEAUM or FRE separately reported. Core EPS used as DE proxy.

FIRM-012_STEP.notes: March fiscal year-end requires mapping. Good coverage on FEAUM and AUM. FRE/ANI data available for FY2023.

FIRM-013_PGHN.notes: Swiss-listed. Reports financials in CHF, AUM in USD. No FEAUM disclosure. EBIT margin used as FRE margin proxy. Cross-currency calculation complicates fee rate.

FIRM-014_HLNE.notes: March fiscal year-end. Strong data coverage. Reports FEAUM, FRE, and non-GAAP EPS clearly.

FIRM-015_ICP.notes: March fiscal year-end. Reports AUM in USD, financials in GBP. FMC operating margin used as FRE proxy. No standalone FRE metric.

FIRM-016_BPT.notes: Fee-paying AUM in EUR, financials in GBP. ECP acquisition in H2 2024 creates significant pro forma adjustments. Limited organic FRE data.

FIRM-017_VINP.notes: BRL-reporting LatAm firm. Compass combination in 2024 breaks YoY comparability. Significant FX headwind (BRL -21.5% vs USD in 2024).

FIRM-018_TKO.notes: EUR-reporting. Good coverage on fee-paying AUM, FRE margin, and fee rate. Core FRE used as primary profitability metric.

key_observations: CVC (FIRM-010) and Vinci Compass (FIRM-017) have the most data gaps due to recent IPO/combination respectively
Man Group (FIRM-011) uses a hedge fund business model that does not map cleanly to alt-manager conventions (no FEAUM, no FRE)
Non-USD reporters (CVC, Bridgepoint, ICG, Partners Group, Tikehau, Vinci Compass) require FX conversions that introduce noise
Firms with March fiscal year-ends (StepStone, Hamilton Lane, ICG) are mapped to the most recent complete fiscal year
Hamilton Lane (FIRM-014) has the cleanest data coverage among Tier 2 firms due to clear US alt-manager style reporting
Partners Group (FIRM-013) has exceptionally high operating margins (61%+ EBIT) reflecting its scaled direct investment model
BRL depreciation in 2024 (-21.5%) significantly distorts Vinci Compass's USD-denominated metrics
